library_intro: Songs, scales & arpeggios, studies, and your own imports — all in one place. Filter by kind or grade, star what you're working on, then open one to read, hear, and practice it.
library_cant_find: Can't find your song? Add it yourself
meta_library_description: Your piano scores on this device — search, star, and practice every piece, and import your own MusicXML.
home_library_blurb: Thousands of pieces — scales and arpeggios, studies, and familiar tunes — plus anything you import. Search, filter by grade, star your favourites, then open one to read, hear, and practise it note by note.
meta_home_title: Plinky — piano practice in your browser
meta_home_description: Practice piano in your browser with a MIDI keyboard or your computer keyboard — sight-reading, rhythm, tempo, ear-training, and loop drills, with your scores kept on your device.
meta_play_description_fallback: Practice a piece with your MIDI piano or computer keyboard
meta_play_description: Practice "{title}" in your browser — sight-read and play it with your MIDI or computer keyboard.
meta_play_description_by: Practice "{title}" by {composer} in your browser — sight-read and play it with your MIDI or computer keyboard.
meta_daily_description: Today's piece — the same for everyone — graded and shareable
meta_ear_description: Train your ear: hear a note and play it back
meta_fingering_description: Choose your own fingering and compare it to a comfortable one
meta_import_description: Add a MusicXML file to your library — it stays on your device
meta_grades_description: Your grade on plinky's practice ladder, and the next one to reach
meta_scores_description: Every piece on this device — rendered, played, and graded
meta_tracks_description: Ordered practice paths — graded progressions and timed routines
meta_sprint_description: Fresh notes every run — sight-read and be graded
you_intro: How you're getting on — your grade and skill, what to practise next, and how you've been doing.
meta_you_description: Your grade, skill, and practice progress on plinky
grades_intro: Each grade is a pool of pieces at one difficulty. Play a couple of them well to reach a grade — so your standing follows what you can play, not how long you grind — then keep going to earn its stars and climb your skill rating. It's your own milestone, not an official certificate.
grades_skill_help: Rises as you play harder pieces well — no ceiling, just keep climbing.
refresh_why: Pieces resurface on a widening schedule, so what you've learned actually sticks.
grades_all_fresh: All fresh — nothing to review right now.
meta_review_description: Refresh the pieces that are fading, one at a time
grades_competitive_help: Pieces you stop practising fade from your grade until you refresh them.
grades_start_play: Play a piece from the library
grades_start_hand: Set your hand size for tailored fingering
discover_intro: Meet the app one feature at a time — no rush, nothing locked.
discover_ear: Train your ear in any piece
discover_fingering: Work out the fingering in any piece
discover_compose: Record your own tune in Compose
discover_import: Import a score of your own
grade_about_1: Five-finger tunes that stay in one hand position — steady quarter and half notes, nothing to stretch for.
grade_about_2: A little hand movement and simple two-hand coordination; mostly steps with the odd small skip.
grade_about_3: Both hands working together more, slightly wider reaches, and the first dotted rhythms and eighth-note runs.
grade_about_4: Position shifts, simple chords, and phrases that ask each hand to do something of its own.
grade_about_5: Faster passages, fuller chords, and accidentals that pull you out of the home key.
grade_about_6: Independent hands throughout, broader leaps, and ornaments or syncopation to hold in time.
grade_about_7: Demanding two-hand textures, quick position changes, and dense rhythms at a real tempo.
grade_about_8: Concert-level pieces — rapid runs, big stretches, and the full range of the keyboard.
coach_play_modes: Tip: switch to Ear or Fingering to work this piece a different way.
transpose_caption: Changes the notes you play, not just the view
forgiving_hint: Don't stop for a missed note — playing the next one moves the score along.
rotate_hint: Turn your phone sideways for a bigger keyboard.
treadmill_hint: Read the piece as one line that scrolls as you play, instead of stacked rows.
hand_size_instruction: tap your thumb key, then your pinky — or play them on a MIDI piano
home_eyebrow: Free · in your browser · nothing to install
home_keyboard_hint: Tap a key — that's a plink.
home_intro: Free, in-browser piano practice — no account, nothing to install. Connect a MIDI keyboard or use your computer keys, then drill sight-reading, rhythm, tempo, and ear training, or practice any score. Every run is graded, and your scores and progress stay on your device.
perf_label: Each note placed by timing, ringed in red if the key was wrong, dashed if you hesitated
share_grid_label: Accuracy, timing and flow across six moments of the run
settings_mastery_threshold: Mark a score learned at grade
settings_mastery_help: A score counts as learned once a run reaches this grade, which schedules it for review. Lower the bar to mark scores learned sooner.
settings_decay_gentle_help: Your grades never drop — pieces just lose their shine until you refresh them. The kind default.
settings_decay_competitive_help: Neglected pieces can lapse, so a grade — and your skill rating — can actually slip. For bragging rights: a ⚔️ badge shows you're playing this way.
settings_review_cap_help: How many due pieces a review session offers in one go, so it stays a finishable batch rather than a pile.
play_sprint_blurb: Fresh notes every run — play as many as you can before the timer runs out.
play_compose_blurb: Improvise freely and Plinky captures every note, sketching your playing onto a staff you can share or export as MusicXML or MIDI.
meta_compose_description: Improvise at the keyboard and Plinky sketches the notation — share your take or download it as MIDI or MusicXML
compose_intro: Play freely on a MIDI keyboard, your computer keys, or the keys below — Plinky records every note and sketches it onto the staff. Count in to play along to the metronome for tidier notation, set a checkpoint to keep the good part and retry the rest, then share your take or download it. Open a MIDI or MusicXML file to pick up where you left off on another device.
compose_staff_label: A staff sketch of your improvisation
compose_staff_empty: Play a few notes and they'll appear here.
compose_recording_idle: Recording — play anything and every note is captured
compose_sketch_note: The audio is exactly what you played; the staff is an approximate sketch, snapped to a grid so it reads as notation.
compose_open_error: Couldn't read that file. Try a MIDI (.mid) or MusicXML (.musicxml, .mxl) file.
daily_intro: A new piece every day, the same for everyone — a piano take on the daily puzzle. Play today's challenge, get graded, and share your result grid.
settings_subtitle: Preferences are saved on this device.
settings_note_hints: Show the next note to play
settings_note_hints_help: A wrong key always flashes red. This sets when the correct key is lit up — keeping it hidden makes you read the music.
settings_hand_size_hint: Set your reach to personalize the suggested fingering. With one hand, set just that hand.
settings_get_help: Get help or report a bug
settings_reset_help: Erase everything on this device — your scores, progress, and settings. This can't be undone, so download a backup from the Library first if you want to keep your scores.
tracks_intro: Ordered practice paths through the catalogue — scales, arpeggios, and pieces in a sensible sequence. Follow a path downward; everything stays unlocked, so the order is just a suggestion and the markers show what you've already set a score on.
meta_assignments_description: Build a practice assignment — an ordered list of pieces and exercises — and share it as a file or a link
assignments_intro: Build a practice assignment — an ordered list of pieces and exercises, each with an optional target tempo and note — then share it as a file or a link. Whoever opens it gets it as a track; their progress stays on their own device.
assignments_received_heading: An assignment was shared with you
assignments_received_detail: "{name}" — {count} items. Import it to add it to your tracks.
assignments_search_placeholder: Search pieces and exercises to add…
assignments_empty_basket: Search above and add pieces or exercises to build the list.
assignments_yours_empty: No assignments yet — build one above, or import a file or link.
assignments_appear_on_tracks: Saved assignments appear as paths on
assignments_save_failed: Couldn't save — this device's storage may be full.
assignments_link_copied: Share link copied to the clipboard.
home_assignments_blurb: Work through a sequence of pieces — a teacher's, or one you build yourself — as a guided practice path, with each step graded as you clear it.
progress_intro: Your days practiced and notes played — tracked privately on this device. Finish a run in any mode to add to it, then share your progress grid.
progress_empty: No practice logged yet — finish a run to start tracking.
progress_grid_label: Accuracy, timing and flow across your recent sessions
import_intro: Drop a MusicXML file to add it to your library — render it, hear it, and practice it like any other piece. Most notation apps (MuseScore, Finale, Sibelius, Flat) export MusicXML.
import_local_note: Scores you add stay on this device and are never uploaded. Clearing your browser data removes them, so
import_drop_here: Drop a MusicXML file here, or click to browse
midi_unsupported_keyboard: This browser does not expose the Web MIDI API. Use Chrome, Edge, or Firefox on desktop or Android — or play with your computer keyboard below.
midi_badge_disconnected: No piano connected — play with these keys
ghost_track_label: Race: you at note {you} of {total}, ghost at note {ghost}
sprint_intro: Sight-reading drills with fresh notes every run — read the phrase at sight, play it on your keyboard, and see how you're graded on accuracy, timing, and flow.
play_not_found: That score isn't on this device.
ear_piece_progress: Play it back by ear — note {index} of {total}
ear_piece_done: Phrase complete! 🎉 Slide to the next bars.
ear_piece_empty: No melody in these bars — try the next ones.
fingering_hint: Pick a finger for the highlighted note — tap below or press 1–5. Click a note to revisit it.
fingering_color_legend: As you go: green flows easily, amber works, red is an awkward reach.
fingering_reconsider: The notes highlighted in amber would play easier with the suggested finger:
window_staff_label: The whole piece, with the bars you're fingering highlighted
focus_strip_label: The bars you're playing now, above the keyboard
fingering_empty: Nothing to finger in this passage — try the other hand or the next bars.
fingering_saved_hint: Your fingering is saved for this piece.
fingering_legend: Top: the note. Middle: your finger. Bottom: a comfortable suggestion.
keyboard_hint_no_piano: No piano? Play with your computer keyboard:
keyboard_hint_shift: shift both hands an octave — current offset
keymap_help: No piano? These keys play the notes. Click a note, then press the key you want it on.
import_no_notes: No playable notes found — is this valid MusicXML?
import_read_error: That file could not be read — try choosing it again.
import_save_failed: Could not save the score — it may exceed this device's storage.
score_load_error: This score couldn't be displayed — the MusicXML may be malformed.
backup_intro: Your scores are saved only on this device — {count} so far. Download a backup, or import a bundle (a backup of your own, or a set shared by a teacher).
backup_import_error: That file could not be imported.
midi_debug_intro: Connect a digital piano and confirm note events arrive in the browser.
midi_debug_unsupported: This browser does not expose the Web MIDI API. Use Chrome, Edge, or Firefox on desktop or Android — Safari (macOS and iOS) is not supported.
